conclusion.summary: The portable backend now covers the reconstructed core flows, but the original repositories contain UI, content-management and provider integrations that are not yet reproduced.

preserved: Separate public and personal chatbot responsibilities
Four, five and nine phase systems
Route matching and ordered route steps
Events and vacancies with saved items
PostgreSQL persistence without Supabase SDK coupling

gaps.status: interfaces exist, live ingestion and retrieval remain
roles exist; prompt versioning and management UI remain
core services exist; operational UI and aggregate APIs remain
adapter boundaries exist; live event, vacancy, retrieval and LLM providers remain
response contracts exist; frontend behavior remains
no portable email or notification provider yet

antiDriftRules: Every new release updates the parity matrix.
Public coach may not own personal phase or profile mutations.
No provider SDK imports in domain or flow packages.
All new persistent behavior requires repository and migration tests.
UI work must consume the existing contracts instead of duplicating business rules.
Live provider integrations remain behind adapter interfaces.

nextBuildPlan.scope: Trusted sources, FAQ ingestion and retrieval
Frontend shell for public chat, profile and personal chat
Backoffice UI, prompt controls, alerts and statistics
Live event, vacancy, LLM, search and notification providers
